# Lab culture

::: {.callout-note}
## Implicit code of conduct
Critique ideas and code, not people. Feedback should be respectful and actionable.
:::

## Before autonomous work

- **Intellectual honesty** about design limits; avoid overstating identification.
- **Respect and collaboration** even in fast debate.
- **Progressive transparency** — version reproducible decisions unless legally restricted.

### Day-to-day communication

1. Post short status if parallel tasks stall (>48h) and someone is waiting.
2. Prefer reproducible artifacts (tables, figures on disk) over one-off screenshots.
3. Be specific in requests (“validate date logic before merge?” not “can you look at everything?”).

## Resolving scientific/technical disagreements

| Situation | Suggested flow |
| --- | --- |
| Variable transform dispute | Open an issue + minimal comparison branch `compare/<topic>`. |
| Authorship / release priority | Document roles and author order in the planning issue tied to the product. |
| Ethics or sensitive data uncertainty | Pause public release; escalate via agreed institutional channels. |

## Inclusive language

Use neutral or explicit wording; many readers are international even when the project is Spanish-first.
